feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA vs. ECC : Key Changes Every Security Consultant Should Know
entry.content:
As a security consultant, not a functional expert, I’m outlining the key differences between SAP S/4HANA and SAP ECC. This overview highlights important changes that are useful when handling security-related tasks.

1st is Business Partners.

BP/Business Partners is not a new concept, it was there in ECC system as well, I remember using it for a particular CRM implementation, where we had multiple relationships to be defined for external partners. But most of the companies still relied on maintaining vendor and customer master data separately. Now with S/4 all the master data is maintained in BP. If you ever do the brownfield implementation, you will see XK01 etc (vendor and customer master data related) tcodes are obsolete and SU25 will suggest to replace it with tcode BP.

This goes with the fact that an external entity can have multiple relations with a business. For instance, Microsoft may act as a vendor for SAP by providing Office 365 solutions while also being a customer using SAP’s ERP system. In Business Partner (BP), these two roles are defined separately, but the core master data for Microsoft remains centralized, ensuring consistency across different relationships.

2nd is Lesser no of Tables

SAP S/4HANA has a reduced number of tables compared to the ECC system, but this applies specifically to the S/4HANA solution, not the HANA database itself. If an ECC system is connected to a HANA database, it will still retain the larger number of tables from ECC.

For example, FI and CO tables have been consolidated into a single table, ACDOCA (Universal Journal), serving as a single source of truth. Additionally, summary tables have been eliminated due to HANA’s column level in-memory storage, enabling faster analytical processing.

3rd is Universal Journal – ACDOCA (Single source of truth)

In ECC solution, FI (Financial Accounting module) and CO (Controlling/Management Accounting) used to have different tables. With S/4HANA, all accounting related postings are posted in one single table called ACDOCA, also called universal journal. Within the same entry posted to ACDOCA, amount can be split and be associated with different cost/profit centers.

4th is Material ledger

If companies are using S/4HANA for their inventory management, using material Ledger is a must. Material ledger is just like BP, which was there in ECC system, but companies didn’t use it much. The material ledger in S/4HANA supports up to 3 currencies.

5th is Foreign Trade

The foreign trade functionality now cannot be used in FI module. If company implementing S/4HANA is into foreign trade operations, they would need to implement SAP GTS. (Global Trade Services).

6th is Embedded Analytics

SAP S/4HANA comes with Embedded Analytics, allowing companies to access real-time insights without needing a separate BW system—perfect for those who don’t require highly customized reports. Key benefits include:

  • Cost Saving - Reduced expenses on licenses, infrastructure, and maintenance.
  • Real-Time Data – Instant access to live business information without replication delays.
  • No Data Duplication – Eliminates redundancy by analyzing data directly within S/4HANA.

7th is Embedded GRC

Just like analytics, S/4HANA gives an option to install in embedded mode. Embedded GRC, just like embedded analytics have same pros – lesser cost, no data duplication and real time analysis. I will cover more about embedded GRC and how it differs from standalone GRC installation in my next blog.

 

I've covered the key points, though not everything. However, this should give security professionals a solid starting point for S/4HANA implementations and interviews

entry.title: Dynamic select query using source syntax
entry.content:
Introduction:

This blog intends dynamic fields or table selection in select query based on the condition. My requirement is to show vendor or customer details based on T-code. A traditional approach is to use conditional statement (IF .. ELSE)  to determine fields, table and where condition. However, there is an alternative and dynamic approach by utilizing source syntax which eliminates reductant code

Step 1: Declare type as below image.

Step 2: Declare internal table as common for both vendor or customer details.
Note: Inline declaration in select query is not possible in source syntax

Step 3: Map the structure(ls_source_syntax) based on T-Code

  1. Column - Based on T-code map the required fields.
  2. Source - Based on T-Code map the required table.
  3. Where – based on T-Code map the selection option

Step 4: Dynamically fields, table, where get mapped based on T-code as shown in the image

Case 1: T-Code: ZLIFNR – Vendor details for particular vendor.

Step1: Pass the input and execute

Output:

 

Case 2: T-Code: ZKUNNR – Customer details

Step1: Pass the input and execute

Output:

Conclusion:

With the source syntax, we can construct select query dynamically based on our requirement to make more flexible instead of traditional approach.

entry.title: AI in My Home enhances efficiency, accuracy, and decision-making for SAP users
entry.content:
Business AI offers numerous benefits that can transform organisations by enhancing efficiency, accuracy, and decision-making. AI allows business users to focus on more strategic, creative, or high-value work, increasing productivity. Over the past few years, we have seen the growth of AI within SAP products, including My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. In this blog, I will discuss how AI enhances SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition My Home and look at future* AI - driven features.  

In the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release, My Home introduced personalised app recommendations powered by SAP Business AI. By analysing business users' history, behaviours, location, and authorisations, it recommended personalised applications to the business user. These recommendations make business users more efficient by allowing them to add these recommended applications to their app favourites. It also introduces them to applications that they may not be aware of which in return improves their productivity.

 

My Home Intelligent App Recommendations

 

Based on these intelligent app recommendations, My Home now intelligently generates Insights cards from these applications and pre delivers them for business users. Insights cards display valuable business information about business processes. They provide analytical information at glance so that business users can take action immediately instead of having to search for information. This also makes interactions with SAP systems more intuitive for new users, who may know what they need to do, but not necessarily know what application they need to find the business data. 

 

Intelligent generation of pre-delivered Insights cards

 

With the success of these intelligent recommendations, we are now focused on how we can further enhance the user experience with the help of AI. With the 2408 release we pre delivered Insights cards to business users but received feedback that we could improve the experience of adding more Insights cards to My Home. Traditionally, Insights cards are added from supporting applications. Identifying the applications that can provide the information that business users require can be time consuming. Not only that, the type of applications that support adding Insights cards can be difficult to find. The business user not only has to identify the application, but they also have to apply certain filters to the data to ensure that the Insights card displays the information they require for it to be useful. We are currently working on a solution where business users can create Insights cards using natural language. They can describe their desired information without having to find the appropriate application and navigating to it.  

 

My Home Smart Personalisation feature

 

Influence My Home Smart Personalisation feature 

This Smart Card generation AI driven feature is available for Beta Testing with the 2502 release. At SAP, we're dedicated to delivering the best possible experience for our customers. That's why we're inviting you to participate in our Beta Testing Program , where you can provide valuable feedback on this new feature and help us refine it  before its official release. By taking part in beta testing, you'll have the opportunity to explore the latest functionality before it's available to the wider customer base, and your input can directly impact the quality and usability of the features. 

 

* Roadmap statement: Please note that some of the content in this blog is about unreleased solutions from SAP. SAP reserves the right to change its development priorities at any time, so please do not make any purchase decisions based on what you read here.

entry.title: Analysis of the name sequence when double-clicking on the contact person's PF in a sales order
entry.content:
Analysis of the name sequence when double-clicking on the contact person's partner function in a sales order.

Sometimes, the sequence of names displays the customer's name first and the contact person's name last.

 

Sometimes, the sequence of names displays the contact person’s name first and the customer's name last.

 


Analysis: Set break point at FM SD_PARTNER_ADDR_DIALOG_INTERN, in this case, system then call FM ADDRESS_INTO_PRINTFORM.

 

The name sequence get determined by each address layout. When the address layout is 011, we can see that the customer's name is listed first.

 

When the address layout is 022, we can see that the contact person’s name is listed first.

 

For different address layout, you can refer to this help portal. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/6dee8af7a26e482388c895f2b9f1e605.html?version=2502.500

 

The address layout key is determined by the receiver's country. By using transaction code OY07, we can identify the country for which the layout key is defined.

entry.title: Shape the Future of the Next-Generation Inbox
entry.content:
Attention My Inbox Users! 

Welcome to the future of task management with SAP Task Center, the next-generation inbox designed to streamline your workflow and keep you organized. If you use My Inbox within SAP Build Process Automation or SAP S/4HANA, your input is vital for making SAP Task Center truly exceptional. 

What is SAP Task Center? 

SAP Task Center is a unified inbox for task management across multiple SAP and non-SAP solutions. It aims to provide a centralized hub where you can manage all your tasks efficiently, ensuring nothing slips through the cracks. 

Why Your Feedback Matters 

As a user of My Inbox within SAP Build Process Automation or SAP S/4HANA, your feedback is crucial for our development process. By sharing your experiences - the good, the bad, and everything in between - you can directly shape the evolution of SAP Task Center. This collaborative effort ensures that the next-generation inbox is tailored to your specific needs and workflows.

How You Can Help

Participate in our survey and let us know what you think about My Inbox. Your insights will help us build a tool that truly understands and meets your workflow requirements.

Survey: Help Shape the Future of SAP Task Center

Together, we can make SAP Task Center an inbox that not only meets but exceeds your expectations. Your time and contributions are greatly appreciated.

If you’re curious to learn more about SAP Task Center, check out the SAP Task Center Product Page.

Thank you for being a part of this journey. Let’s build something remarkable together!

entry.title: New: All 3 Process Flows available by default in SAP Signavio Process Insights, Discovery Edition!
entry.content:
What’s new?

Customers can now request a comprehensive analysis with the latest version of SAP Notes, and they will always get all three Process Flows (and hence don't need to choose anymore).

Which 3 Process Flows are available?

  • Supplier invoice issuing (with purchase order) to FI-AP clearing (KPPURCH460): Measures the number of supplier invoices created in reference to a purchase order in the reference period and shows how many of them reached predefined stages of the process and how long on average this progression took. Learn More.
  • Sales billing document creation to FI-AR clearing (KPSD000260): Measures the number of sales billing documents created in the reference period for which a corresponding FI document and customer payment are expected and shows how many of them reached predefined stages of the process and how long on average this progression took. Lean More.
  • Purchase order item creation (with account assignment) to invoice receipt creation (KPPURCH268): Measures the number of purchase order items created in the reference period for which a goods receipt as well as an invoice receipt are expected and shows how many of them reached predefined stages of the process and how long on average this progression took.​ Learn More.

How to extract the data and request Discovery Edition?

The instructions in the How-to Guide supports customers through the process.

Customers can get all information and request their free SAP Signavio Process Insights, Discovery Edition via our landing page: https://url.sap/discovery-edition 

Recommended blogs:

  • Rene's Blog Series of SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition
  • Get started with SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition
  • What you must know about SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition

entry.title: Introducing SAP Application Extension Methodology version 2.3
entry.content:
What’s New for SAP Application Extension Methodology

With the SAP Application Extension Methodology version 2.3, we have made some changes to the technology-agnostic extension tasks and refined the Extension Technology Mapping. For example, we introduced new extension tasks around the topic of Event-Driven Architecture.

If you are still using an older version of the Technology Mapping, I highly recommend checking out our new version and to learn about the latest innovations and technologies on SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Technology Platform.

 

Extension Architecture Guide

Our Methodology is part of the SAP BTP Guidance Framework. With this Framework, we provide a central access point for architects, developers, and administrators to build and run enterprise-grade solutions on SAP BTP.

We now offer a dedicated topic on “Getting Started with Extensibility”. This guide provides a simplified introduction to extending SAP’s ERP offerings. It outlines the SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensibility model with SAP Business Technology Platform, which applies to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (GROW with SAP), SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (RISE with SAP), and SAP S/4HANA.

Getting Started with Extensibility

With SAP Build, you can create extensions that run on-stack in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA, and/or side-by-side on SAP BTP.

Depending on your use cases and the capabilities required, you can choose a flexible combination of on-stack and side-by-side extensions.

 

Next Steps:

Learn more about the SAP Application Extension Methodology on the SAP Help Portal.
Learn more about the Extension Architecture Guide on the SAP Help Portal.

Feel free to like, comment and ask your questions!

Talk to you soon,
Marco

Product Manager SAP Business Technology Platform
Join me online: https://www.linkedin.com/in/marcomatha/

entry.title: Where can I learn processes and tools for effective sourcing within the SAP S/4HANA environment?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring Sourcing in S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
This course provides participants with an overview of the sources of supply available in SAP S/HANA, and presents the processes and tools necessary for effective sourcing within the SAP S/4HANA environment.

Learning objectives
​After completing this learning journey, Business Users will be able to use special purchasing information records, the source list, and quota arrangements, and describe the different options for determining the source of supply.
This course equips users with the skills needed to perform sourcing processes and drive organizational efficiency.

Prerequisites 

  • Exploring Contract Management in SAP S/4HANA Sourcing and Procurement

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Announcement: Removal of Belize Theme of SAP Fiori
entry.content:
Executive summary

  • Automatic move to Horizon theme in SAP Fiori
  • Action is needed for custom themes based on Belize.

Themes in SAP Fiori

SAP Fiori is a design system that provides a consistent user experience for SAP software across platforms and devices. It is at the core of SAP’s product experience and design strategy. An essential element defining many aspects of the visual design is the theme. The latest Horizon theme is an evolution of the SAP Fiori design system and offers a modern, fresh look, and an improved user experience helping users to focus on their daily work. 

Belize theme removal

A previous version of Horizon is the Belize theme, introduced with SAP Fiori 2.0. Belize is deprecated as of SAPUI5 1.120 (see Deprecated Themes and Libraries) and SAP recommends to move to the current Horizon theme.

 

Existing usages of Belize will be automatically redirected to the Horizon theme.
With SAPUI5/OpenUI5 1.136 the UI5 controls will not work any longer with the Belize theme.

For more details on Belize in SAP S/4HANA and other products, please see Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori themes Belize and Blue Crystal from @ThomasReiss. Other UI technologies are affected in the same way:

  • Web Dynpro ABAP: SAP_UI 8.16 & SAP_BASIS 9.16
  • SAP GUI for HTML: SAP Kernel 9.16 used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508
  • WebClient UI: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508

With this focus on the Horizon theme SAP can deliver innovations faster. Also, runtime performance is expected to gain from a smaller CSS payload.

Custom themes based on Belize

Using the UI theme designer customers can apply their own branding by adapting a standard SAP Fiori theme: Customers may replace the logo or change the main branding colors of the UI by replacing the default brand and base colors of the theme. When creating a custom theme, an SAP standard theme (such as Morning Horizon) is selected as base theme.

Existing custom themes based on Belize need to be migrated before a mentioned UI technology version is used. Details on this migration are given in Migrate Your Themes with Ease and SAP Help: Migrating Themes on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment.

entry.title: Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori Themes Belize and Blue Crystal
entry.content:
The SAP Fiori visual themes Belize and Blue Crystal have been deprecated for many years. Now the time has come to optimize our UI technology by removing these themes. We plan to remove them in the next releases of products which allow users to choose multiple themes, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA (on-premise) and SAP Integrated Business Planning.

Over the years SAP Fiori has evolved, introducing new visual themes along the way – most recently the Horizon theme. The Horizon theme not only looks modern and appealing, it also provides the highest level of support for accessibility, as well as the most flexibility for customers to create their own custom themes.

Horizon is the visual theme used by all our products in the SAP Business Suite. Figures 1 – 3 below show some examples of Horizon in our products.

We strongly recommend that all users use the Horizon theme to ensure an optimal and consistent user experience.

 

Removal of Belize and Blue Crystal

The visual themes Belize and Blue Crystal will be removed from SAP products with their next releases, starting in the second half of 2025. This means that the upcoming releases of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP Integrated Business Planning, as well as further products which currently include one or both of these themes, will no longer provide them as options.

After upgrading, existing usages of Belize or Blue Crystal will be automatically redirected to the Horizon theme.

If you have created your own custom theme based on Belize or Blue Crystal using the UI theme designer, you will need to migrate this to an available theme. Our strong recommendation is to base the new version on the Horizon theme.

For more information about the UI technologies affected, and how to migrate custom themes, have a look at this blog post by Oliver Graeff:

  • Announcement: Removal of Belize theme of SAP Fiori.

Additional Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Important: If you use a custom theme and have set up the UI Theme Designer connection to your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with a communication arrangement on SAP Business Technology Platform Neo, make sure to move it to Cloud Foundry before you upgrade. Otherwise the automatic redirect to Horizon will not work. Also, future innovations will require the communication arrangement to use Cloud Foundry, so doing this move makes sense anyway. Here is a link to the documentation for this:

  • Integrating UI Theme Designer – SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Environment.

 

Examples of SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon

You can see some examples for Horizon in figures 1 – 3: for SAP Start and SAP Mobile Start, for SAP S/4HANA Cloud (the images show SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502) and SAP SuccessFactors. For further examples, have a look at these blog posts:

  • SAP Fiori Update: More Products with Horizon, Entry Point strategy, UX news for SAP S/4HANA and More.
  • SAP User Experience Update: Yet More Products with the New Look driven by SAP Fiori.
  • Improvements Coming to the Horizon Visual Theme for SAP Fiori.
  • SAP UX Innovations in Public Cloud Products, Joule, AI, Entry Points, Apple Vision Pro and more.
  • SAP UX Latest 2024 Innovations in Public Cloud: Joule, GenAI and More.
  • SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More) – along with parts 2 – 7 which are linked therein.

Figure 1: The SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon for SAP Start and SAP Mobile Start. ALT Text: the image shows a screenshot of SAP Start with the Horizon theme, three iPhones with Horizon (one dark and one light showing SAP Mobile start, one showing an iPhone start screen with a notification from SAP Mobile STart ), and an Apple Watch with a notification from SAP Mobile Start and Horizon.

Figure 2: The SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. ALT Text: an image showing two sceens with Morning Horizon (the My Home page and Monitor Purchase Order Items analytical list page) and two screens with the same apps with Evening Horizon, plus the text “Also available for accessibility: high contrast light and dark themes”.

Figure 3: Examples of SAP SuccessFactors applications with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon. ALT Text: Three examples showing both web and mobile: on the left the Home Page, in the middle Team Goals, on the right Opportunity Marketplace showing many cards, each displaying an opportunity.

To find out more about the Horizon theme, have a look at the SAP Fiori for web design guidelines:

  • Theming.

entry.title: Do you require knowledge for SAP authorization concept for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring the Authorization Concept for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite learning journey.

Overview
Learn the fundamental knowledge of the authorization concept for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite to secure application-level access to your SAP environments (Private Cloud and On-Premise). The role maintenance, as well as details about the elements, strategies, and tools of the authorization concept will be covered.

Learning objectives
This course will prepare you to:

  • Learn about the elements, strategies, and tools of the SAP authorization concept for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite.
  • Understand the terminology of authorizations for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite.
  • Create and assign authorizations using the Role Maintenance
  • Learn about the basic settings of the Role Maintenance

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Informationen und Updates zu SAP Preferred Success 2025
entry.content:
NEWS 2025 - Informationen und Updates zu SAP Preferred Success

"Digital Adoption" ist in aller Munde – aber was bedeutet das konkret für Unternehmen? Mein Kollege Thomas Jenewein hat in seinem aufschlussreichen Blogbeitrag die verschiedenen Aspekte und betroffenen Bereiche dieses Konzepts anschaulich dargelegt.

Ein Kernaspekt der digitalen Adoption ist die Anpassungsfähigkeit an neue technologische Möglichkeiten. Anders ausgedrückt: Es geht darum, wie Unternehmen neue digitale Werkzeuge und Technologien effektiv nutzen und in ihren Arbeitsalltag integrieren. Diese Herausforderung stellt viele Organisationen vor komplexe Aufgaben – und genau hier setzt SAP Preferred Success an.

Wie genau unterstützt SAP Preferred Success Unternehmen bei ihrer digitalen Transformation?

SAP Preferred Success ist weit mehr als nur ein Supportmodell – es ist ein umfassender Begleiter für Unternehmen auf ihrer Cloud-Reise mit SAP-Lösungen. Der Service bietet nicht nur Unterstützung bei der initialen Implementierung, sondern steigert langfristig die Produktivität und Effizienz von Organisationen in der Cloud.

Im Mittelpunkt steht ein dedizierter Customer Success Manager, der in regelmäßigen Abstimmungsmeetings einen maßgeschneiderten Erfolgsplan erstellt und dessen Umsetzung begleitet.

Dieser Plan bedient sich aus einem großen Katalog verschiedener Services, die auf die spezifischen Bedürfnisse des jeweiligen Unternehmens zugeschnitten werden. Dazu gehören Solution Launch Checks und Success Checks, die eine optimale Konfiguration der SAP-Lösungen sicherstellen und Verbesserungspotenziale aufdecken.

Ein Highlight ist die detaillierte Release-Begleitung, die tiefe Einblicke in neue Software-Versionen und deren Auswirkungen bietet. Dies ermöglicht eine proaktive Planung und erleichtert die Anpassung an neue Funktionalitäten.

Zur Förderung der kontinuierlichen Weiterbildung beinhaltet das Programm fünf Lizenzen für den SAP Learning Hub. Diese ermöglichen den Zugang zu gezielten Schulungsprogrammen, die auf die spezifischen Anforderungen des Unternehmens abgestimmt sind.

SAP Preferred Success bietet zudem erweiterten Support mit verbesserten Service Level Agreements. Dies gewährleistet schnellere Reaktionszeiten und effizientere Problemlösungen. Bei komplexen Herausforderungen stehen dem Unternehmen Fachexperten mit fundiertem Know-how zur Seite.

Insgesamt zielt SAP Preferred Success darauf ab, Unternehmen die notwendigen Werkzeuge, das erforderliche Wissen und die benötigte Unterstützung zu bieten, um ihre digitale Transformation erfolgreich zu gestalten und den maximalen Nutzen aus ihren SAP-Lösungen zu ziehen.

Eine unabhängige Studie von Forrester Consulting hat die Vorteile von SAP Preferred Success untersucht und quantifiziert. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass Unternehmen, die das Programm nutzen, erhebliche Verbesserungen erzielen konnten. Dazu gehören eine gesteigerte Produktivität, reduzierte Systemausfallzeiten und beschleunigte Einführung und Updates. Die Erkenntnisse aus der Studie unterstreichen den Wertbeitrag von SAP Preferred Success für Unternehmen, die ihre SAP-Investitionen optimal nutzen möchten.

Kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung: Neuerungen bei SAP Preferred Success

SAP Preferred Success entwickelt sich stetig weiter, um mit den sich wandelnden Anforderungen der Kunden und den Fortschritten in der SAP-Technologie Schritt zu halten. In den vergangenen Jahren wurde das Programm nicht nur für eine breitere Palette von SAP-Lösungen ausgerollt, sondern auch um innovative Services erweitert.

Eine Übersicht der SAP-Lösungen, für die SAP Preferred Success verfügbar ist, einschließlich detaillierter Informationen zu jeder Lösung, findet sich hier.

SAP hat mit der Einführung der "Expanded Edition" von Preferred Success einen bedeutenden Schritt nach vorn gemacht. Diese erweiterte Version richtet sich an Unternehmen, die vor komplexeren Herausforderungen stehen oder eine intensivere Betreuung bei der Nutzung ihrer SAP-Lösungen wünschen.

Zusammengefasst bieten die Expanded Editions von SAP Preferred Success eine Reihe von Vorteilen:

  • Tiefgreifenderes Expertenwissen durch einen erweiterten Zugang zu Fachleuten, die ihr umfangreiches SAP Know-How einbringen.
  • Zusätzliche Unterstützung: Neben dem Customer Success Manager steht ein Produktspezialist zur Verfügung, der detaillierte Einblicke bietet.
  • Verbesserte Supportleistungen, um noch schneller und effektiver auf Kundenanfragen reagieren zu können.

Auch zu den Expanded Editions von SAP Preferred Success gibt es eine unabhängige Forrester-Studie, die deren Vorteile für Unternehmen analysiert und bewertet hat. Laut dieser Studie erzielten Unternehmen eine signifikante Effizienzsteigerung. Dies äußerte sich vor allem in einem schnelleren Zugang zu SAP-Expertise, einer deutlichen Reduzierung externer Partnerkosten sowie erheblichen Zeitersparnissen bei der Verwaltung von Anpassungen. Darüber hinaus stellte die Studie eine Verbesserung des internen Fachwissens, eine Beschleunigung von Innovationsprozessen und eine Minderung von Systemrisiken fest.

Neben der Einführung von SAP Preferred Success für zusätzliche SAP-Lösungen und der Erweiterung durch die Expanded Edition für einige der Lösungen, hat SAP Preferred Success auch sein Serviceportfolio erweitert, um den sich wandelnden Bedürfnissen der Kunden gerecht zu werden. Dabei ist zu beachten, dass sich die angebotenen Services je nach SAP-Lösung unterscheiden, um eine möglichst passgenaue Unterstützung zu gewährleisten.

Ein besonderer Schwerpunkt liegt auf den neu eingeführten AI Accelerator Services. Diese innovativen Dienste sind darauf ausgerichtet, Unternehmen bei der Nutzung künstlicher Intelligenz in ihren SAP-Lösungen zu unterstützen und sie auf dem Weg zu einer KI-gestützen, zukunftsorientierten Unternehmensführung zu begleiten. Obwohl die spezifischen Funktionen je nach SAP-Produkt variieren, verfolgen die AI Accelerator Services generell drei Hauptziele:

  1. Innovation: Unterstützung bei der Entwicklung und Implementierung neuer, KI-gestützter Geschäftsmodelle und Prozesse.
  2. Automatisierung: Hilfe bei der Identifizierung und Umsetzung von Automatisierungsmöglichkeiten durch KI, um Effizienz und Produktivität zu steigern.
  3. Prozessoptimierung: Beratung zur Verbesserung bestehender Geschäftsabläufe durch den Einsatz von KI-Technologien.

Beispielsweise könnten die AI Accelerator Services für SAP S/4HANA Kunden dabei unterstützen, intelligente Vorhersagemodelle für die Bestandsoptimierung zu implementieren. Für SAP SuccessFactors-Nutzer könnten sie Hilfestellung bei der Einführung KI-gestützter Rekrutierungsprozesse bieten.

Eine weitere Sparte neuer SAP Preferred Success Services kommt aus dem Bereich Organizational Change Management (OCM). Um dieses Thema abzudecken, das für die Digital Adoption aus menschlicher Sicht unverzichtbar ist, hat das Digital Change and Adoption Team der SAP zwei Services ausgearbeitet:

  • den Transformation Mindset Insights Service sowie
  • den Operational Change & Adoption Readiness Service.

Beide Services nähern sich dem Thema Transformation aus der menschlichen Perspektive, denn der Fakt bleibt: Ohne die Mitarbeiter*innen kann keine Transformation erfolgreich werden.

Der Transformation Mindset Insights Service von SAP hilft Organisationen, ihren Reifegrad in Bezug auf Transformationsfähigkeit zu verstehen und kontinuierlich zu verbessern.

Der Prozess umfasst:

  • Analyse der sechs Mindset Traits, namentlich Resilienz, Systemisches Denken, Kontinuierliche Entwicklung, Cross-Kollaboration, Offenheit für Veränderung und Situative Adaptivität.
  • Datenbasierter Ansatz mit Management-Sicht und Mitarbeiterbefragungen.
  • Workshops zur Erarbeitung einer Roadmap zur Verbesserung des Transformation Mindset Reifegrads.
  • Umsetzung von Maßnahmen in einer mit dem Kunden vereinbarten Aktionsphase.
  • Erneute Erhebung des Reifegrads zur Überprüfung der Effektivität der Maßnahmen.
  • Kontinuierliche Arbeit am Transformation Mindset

So können Organisationen kontinuierlich an ihrem Transformation Mindset arbeiten und das Expertenwissen der SAP bei Bedarf zur Unterstützung heranziehen, um so den neuen Herausforderungen unserer stetig wandelnden Welt einen Schritt vorauszubleiben.

Ein zufriedener Kunde hatte Folgendes zu den Ergebnissen des Transformation Mindset Insights Service zu sagen: „[Der Service] zeigt genau, was wir im letzten Jahr vermutet hatten. Er fängt die Gefühle ein, die mir die Leute hinter verschlossenen Türen mitgeteilt haben. Diese kann ich dem Management nun anhand realer Daten zeigen. Die Ergebnisse spiegeln unsere Situation sehr gut wider.“

Der Operational Change & Adoption Readiness Service richtet sich derweil hauptsächlich an Kunden der Human Capital Management (HCM) Sparte der SAP Lösungen, welche den Reifegrad ihrer OCM Aktivitäten verstehen und verbessern möchten, bevor sie ihre Transformation beginnen.
SAPs OCM Expert*innen evaluieren in einem datenbasierten Vorgehen bestehende OCM Aktivitäten und Konzepte der Kunden und erarbeiten basierend auf den Ergebnissen gemeinsam mit den Kunden Verbesserungsempfehlungen für ihre OCM- und Trainingsplanung. So können Organisationen auch ohne ein ganzes OCM Engagement sicherstellen, dass sie einen gut informierten und effektiven OCM Ansatz für ihre Transformation verfolgen.

Kunden können sich für detaillierte Informationen zu den verfügbaren Services für ihre spezifischen SAP-Lösungen an ihren Customer Success Manager wenden.

Fazit:

SAP Preferred Success entwickelt sich stetig weiter, um den Herausforderungen der digitalen Transformation gerecht zu werden. Mit der Expanded Edition und neuen Services wie den AI Accelerator Services oder Change Management Services unterstreicht SAP sein Ziel, Kunden bei der optimalen Nutzung ihrer SAP-Lösungen zu unterstützen.

Dabei wird individuell auf Kundenbedürfnisse eingegangen und die Wertschöpfung aus SAP-Investitionen verbessert. So bleiben Unternehmen für zukünftige Anforderungen gut gerüstet.

Informieren Sie sich auf SAP Preferred Success, werfen Sie einen Blick auf die ausgesuchten Informationen unten, wenden Sie sich an Ihre SAP-Ansprechperson oder nehmen Sie mit uns Kontakt auf.

Übrigens: Am 25. Juni 2025 werden die BLS AG und die Barmer Krankenversicherung im Rahmen unseres SAP Learning & Adoption Forums in Walldorf über ihre Erfahrungen mit den Services von SAP Preferred Success berichten. Nutzen Sie die Gelegenheit, Fragen zu stellen und in lockerer Atmosphäre den Mehrwert von SAP Preferred Success für Ihre eigene Transformationsreise zu evaluieren. Die Veranstaltung findet hybrid statt. Wir würden uns sehr freuen, Sie vor Ort oder online begrüssen zu dürfen! Hier geht's zur Anmeldung: Registration

Links auf weitere Blogbreiträge und Artikel zu SAP Preferred Success:

Auf Erfolgskurs mit SAP Preferred Success: Einblicke in den Alltag eines Customer Success Partners

Kundenreferenz mit Lufthansa Technik: “Back to standard” – Wie SAP Preferred Success Lufthansa bei der digitalen Adoption unterstützt

SAP Preferred Success: Transforming Challenges into Opportunities for Companies Worldwide in 2024

Aktuelle Kundenreferenzen in MEE:

SAP Preferred Success for S/4HANA

SAP Preferred Success for HCM

SAP Preferred Success für Digital Supply Chain

entry.title: How I Fixed the “GET_BUSINESS_SYSTEM_ERROR” in SAP Migrate Your Data Fiori App
entry.content:
If you’ve been working with SAP’s Migrate Your Data (MYD) Fiori app, you know it’s a powerful tool for transferring data into your S/4HANA system. However, sometimes, even the most straightforward processes can throw you an unexpected curveball.

That’s exactly what happened to us while using the Migrate Your Data app. After uploading data via an XML template, the Simulation process completed successfully, but when we tried to execute the Migrate step, we ran into a roadblock with several error messages.

In this blog post, I’ll walk you through the exact error we encountered, how I troubleshooted it, and how I ultimately fixed the issue by addressing a misconfiguration in the System Landscape Directory (SLD).

Hopefully, this will save you some time if you encounter the same issue.

The Error: What Happened?

The issue occurred after we followed the usual steps of uploading the XML file (which we downloaded from the Fiori app as a template) and populating it with the necessary data. Everything seemed fine during the Simulation process. No errors there! However, when we hit Migrate, the following errors popped up:

  1. System error occurs when calling function module in target system
  2. System error (portion 0000000001, conversion object Z_CUSTOMER_2_NDV, at 122705)
  3. GET_BUSINESS_SYSTEM_ERROR: An error occurred when determining the business system (conversion object Z_CUSTOMER_2_NDV)

Figure 1. Error messages

At first glance, the errors didn’t provide much information, and after a search in SAP Notes, I didn’t find any directly related solutions. These vague error messages left me scratching my head, wondering what went wrong.

Troubleshooting: The Search for Answers

I started by digging into each of the error messages to see if they pointed to any common issues. After some time, it became clear that the root of the problem was related to Business System configuration. Specifically, there was no Business System entry for my S/4HANA system in the System Landscape Directory (SLD).

What is the SLD, and why is it important?

The System Landscape Directory (SLD) is an important component in SAP that holds information about the system landscape, including the system’s technical data, configuration, and communication setup. The Business System entry in the SLD is crucial for the system to recognize and connect with other systems during processes like data migration.

Without a proper Business System entry, SAP can’t identify the correct system to interact with during the migration process, leading to errors like the ones I encountered.

The Solution: Creating the Business System Entry

Once I pinpointed the issue, the fix was relatively straightforward. Here’s how I resolved the problem:

Step 1: Log in to the System Landscape Directory (SLD)

I first logged in to the SLD system through the URL. If you're unsure where your SLD is hosted, it’s typically available at a URL like:

http://<your-sld-server>:<port>/sld/

At this point, you should be able to see your system landscape.

Figure 2. Main SLD screen

Step 2: Create a Business System Entry for Your S/4HANA System

Inside the SLD, I needed to create a new Business System entry for my S/4HANA system. This entry is necessary for SAP to know where to send the data during the migration. Here’s how I did it:

  1. Navigate to the Business Systems section.
  2. Click "Create" to add a new Business System entry.
  3. Fill in the required details like the name of your system, its type (e.g., S/4HANA), and other technical details such as its ID, host, and instance number.
    (You can copy and adjust details from some other existing Business System)
  4. Save the entry. This will add your S/4HANA system as a valid Business System in the SLD.

Figure 3. Business Systems

Step 3: Re-run the Migration

After saving the Business System entry, I went back to the Migrate Your Data Fiori app and tried the migration again. This time, I clicked Migrate, and… success! The process completed without any errors.

Figure 4. Migration completed Successfully

Figure 5. No more Error messages

Why Did This Work?

The issue was that the Business System configuration was missing in the SLD, and as a result, SAP couldn’t determine where to send the data during the migration. By adding the correct Business System entry, I provided the system with the information it needed to communicate with the target system, resolving the errors I encountered.

This step is often overlooked, but it’s crucial for ensuring smooth communication between SAP systems during processes like data migration.

Additional Tips:

While this issue was specific to the Business System configuration, here are a few general tips for troubleshooting similar issues in the future:

  1. Check System Logs: Always check the system logs and trace files for more detailed error messages that might provide more insights.
  2. SAP Notes and Community Forums: If you can't find a solution in SAP Notes, check the SAP Community forums. Other users may have encountered similar problems and found solutions.
  3. Double-check your XML Template: Sometimes the issue might be with the data in your XML template. Make sure that all required fields are filled out correctly, and the format matches the expected template.

Conclusion:

If you’re encountering the GET_BUSINESS_SYSTEM_ERROR error in the Migrate Your Data Fiori app, I hope this blog helps you troubleshoot the issue. The root cause for me was the missing Business System entry in the System Landscape Directory (SLD), which led to the migration failure. Once I created the entry, everything worked as expected.

Have you faced similar errors? Or perhaps you have a different solution? Feel free to share your experience in the comments below. Let’s help each other out!

Tags:

SAP, Fiori App, Migrate Your Data, GET_BUSINESS_SYSTEM_ERROR, S/4HANA, Data Migration, System Error, Business System, SLD, SAP Troubleshooting

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Compatibility Packs
entry.content:
Product Information

Betty Guedez

April 2ND, 2025

SAP S/4HANA Compatibility Packs in a Nutshell

Updated as of release 2023

Let's have a look at a highly relevant topic when transitioning to SAP S/4HANA, especially now that we are in 2025: Compatibility Packs: how to understand them in an easy way, where to get information about them and what the timelines are now in which Compatibility Packs will be operating, depending on the strategy recently released by SAP.

I am Betty Guedez, Product & Engineering Solution Architect at SAP Regional Implementation Group RIG. I feel honored to come back to this community with an SAP strategic topic like this.

If we ask Joule, we get that "... Compatibility Packs in SAP S/4HANA are designed to ensure that certain functionalities from older SAP systems remain available in the new SAP S/4HANA environment. These packs allow customers to continue using specific features (...), providing a bridge during the transition period. They help maintain compatibility and support business continuity while customers adapt to the new system...".

Compatibility Packs are functionalities that were deployed as embedded processes in the SAP ERP and that will remain available in the SAP S/4HANA for a specific period, at the end of which SAP will execute the final stages of the strategy.

In a recent announcement, SAP shared a new timeline for Compatibility Packs. Now, together with Customer Service (CS), Logistics Execution and Transportation (LE-TRA) and Production Planning - Process Industries (PP-PI) (specific solutions that already had their expiration period set for 2030), all RISE with SAP agreements can continue using their Compatibility Packs until 2030 (not 2025 anymore). This is carefully explained in SAP Note 2269324 - Compatibility Scope Matrix for SAP S/4HANA

Figure 1: SAP S/4HANA Compatibility Packs timeline as of January 2025

Compatibility Packs, Readiness Check and Simplification Item Check

As mentioned above, SAP's Compatibility Packs offer customers temporary and limited usage rights to run specific SAP ERP solutions they have licensed on SAP S/4HANA, as outlined in their agreements. It is crucial to know this at the time of thinking about transitioning to SAP S/4HANA. That is why the SAP Readiness Check shows a Compatibility Scope Analysis on its main dashboard, providing information about simplification items related to Compatibility Packs and their status.

Figure 2: SAP Readiness Check Dashboard

In the SAP Readiness Check dashboard, 2 categories under Compatibility Scope are visible: Relevance and Status. By Relevance: each item in the list is related to specific compatibility package ID and some other details like Description, Component and the specific business impact note that must be carefully reviewed. If there is an alternative solution, this detail is also provided.

By selecting Compatibility Scope by Status, you will see information on different alternatives (if available), along with the previously mentioned details.

Figure 3: SAP Readiness Check Compatibility Scope Analysis

Later, during the transition to SAP S/4HANA process, Compatibility Scope details are provided in the SAP Simplification Item Check (SIC), issued in the SAP ERP backend. The procedure to find them is as follows:

Through transactions SA38 or SE38, execute the program /SDF/RC_START_CHECK, and use theRelevance Check to identify what functionalities are currently in use.

Figure 4: SAP Simplification Item Check

Then, analyzing the content of the column labeled "Category", filtering it to include only "Non-strategic" values (according to SAP Note 2931193 - Explanation of Simplification Item Categories) - which is easier after downloading it to an Excel spreadsheet:

Figure 5: Compatibility Packs Analysis after downloaded from SIC

For both cases, SAP Readiness Check and SAP Simplification Item Check, the second analysis for these objects must take place:

  1. Non-strategic Function (alternative exists): Another solution for the functionality exists to fulfill all major requirements expected. Transition must be evaluated and planned. Example: Simplification Item SI13: FIN_CO S4TWL - EC-PCA - Classical Profit Center Accounting Planning, can be replaced by SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning with SAP S/4HANA (SAC), according to SAP Note 2993220 - S4TWL - EC-PCA - Classical Profit Center Accounting Planning
  2. Non-strategic Function (alternative exists with roadmap): An alternative functionality is available in SAP S/4HANA, although the offering varies in scope. Those variations have been included in a roadmap for that alternative. Example: SI8_FIN_General S4TWL - Report Writer / Report Painter in Finance and Controlling, explained in SAP Note 2997574 S4TWL - Report Writer / Report Painter in Finance and Controlling
  3. Non-strategic Function (alternative planned): The successor functionality is not yet available, but there is a roadmap. Example: Simplification Item SI01: CS - S4TWL - Customer Service, SAP Note 2962632 - S4TWL - Customer Service.
  4. Non-strategic Function (no alternative planned): For these, there is currently no replacement planned. One example of this is SI7_FIN_AA - S4TWL - Group Assets. SAP Note 3532580 - Group Assets: Succession solutions contains guidance on how to proceed with this.

Compatibility Packs in the SAP Early Watch Report

As many of you probably know, the SAP Early Watch (aka SAP EWA) is an automatic service at no extra charge, that analyzes the areas of an SAP running instance, providing information on situations (maybe critical or not) and proposing solutions to protect performance and stability of the system, usually issued on a weekly basis. It is an SAP service that has been among us for long time now. For setup steps of this diagnostic, please refer to SAP Note 2520319 - How to access the SAP EarlyWatch Alert (EWA) apps in SAP for Me.

The SAP EWA provides various diagnostics and statistics, like risks that can affect business continuity, critical KPIs per system, bottlenecks, general security alerts and recommendations to improve system performance and operations. SAP EWA also brings risk mitigation strategies and a long list of insights. Additionally, the SAP EWA includes information on the usage of Simplification Items related to Compatibility Packs that are approaching an expiration date in an SAP S/4HANA system.

Written or online, the SAP EWA delivers information about the status of Compatibility Packs in an SAP system. In SAP For Me, once you get to the SAP EWA Solution Finder, after opening the report chapter for the system, a list of the Compatibility Packs in use over the last three months is pulled. For each, a specific SAP Note with background information of the business process and an alternative path forward is provided.

So, if already in an SAP S/4HANA system, the SAP EWA helps track the progress in transitioning to the new solutions when still using Compatibility Packs. With the SAP EWA, following the usage of each Compatibility Item and tracing the progress in replacing the functionality is an ongoing process.

For RISE with SAP customers, the SAP EWA tracks the replacement of Compatibility Packs with the expiration set for 2030, so no messages will be raised for now. For others, the SAP EWA highlights Compatibility Packs expiring at the end of 2025 in red, as a warning that actions must be taken soon.

As a conclusion, I must say that making a decision about what to do next, in terms of transitioning from the old to the new redesigned, improved business processes is something that shall not wait too long. Compatibility Packs bring a unique opportunity to adopt the new innovations available, gain efficiencies by leveraging new and better practices and give business users the time to adapt to them with minimal disruption and in a timely manner.

Get the best of it!

I hope you find reading this blog worthwhile.

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Q1-25 Updates & Highlights
entry.content:
In the past quarter, we continued delivering many more useful features and functions in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, and we achieved great success stories together with our customers. This blog post continues my quarterly series and gives you an overview of some of the highlights. In this edition, I’m covering:

  • Emission Factor Validity Periods and Mass Updates
  • AI-assisted Emission Factor Mapping Enhancement
  • Sankey Chart Improvements
  • Internal Energy Production with Multiple Energy Carriers
  • Item Footprints API
  • Other Release Highlights
  • Open-Source Integration Accelerator
  • Customer Success Story: HARTING Technology Group
  • Intermediate Learning Journey for CCF Calculation

 

Emission Factor Validity Periods and Mass Updates

To streamline the management of emission factors, we've refined the validity period approach within the Manage Emission Factors app. This adjustment enhances the alignment of emission factor references used in consumer apps, simplifying the process of footprint calculations. The validity period has been shifted from the dataset level to the data package level, meaning that each data package now clearly specifies when its emission factors are valid for use. This allows you to easily track the timeframe applicable for accurate footprint assessments. Furthermore, datasets have transitioned from having a validity period to featuring a reference period. The reference period indicates when the data were originally collected, providing essential context for product or activity assessments. Typically, this period is supplied by data providers to ensure representative data usage. Additionally, both templates used for preparing data files for emission factor imports have been revised in line with this change. This update provides more precise and relevant time frames for the use of emission factors, improving the overall accuracy and reliability of footprint calculations. For more information, see: Manage Emission Factors.

With this updated handling of validity periods in the Manage Emission Factors app, the consuming applications Manage Purchased Product Footprints and Manage Waste Footprints have also been adjusted accordingly. Whether you're manually creating footprints or importing them via data files, the app smartly assigns the validity periods of the emission factor data package, ensuring consistency and increasing efficiency in the mapping process. When manually selecting an emission factor reference, the system automatically adopts the validity period from the data package, with the flexibility to adjust the dates as needed. Similarly, when importing through data files, the validity period is copied from the emission factor package unless a specific period is provided, in which case the latter is taken. AI-assisted mappings in the Manage Purchase Products Footprints app also benefit from automatically incorporated validity periods, ensuring seamless adjustments after release.

Another major improvement in the Manage Emission Factors app now enables you to upgrade data packages to the newest version of your data source, ensuring you’re using the most current and most reliable emission factor data in your footprint calculation. This upgrade is crucial for obtaining more precise information, whether it involves new classifications or enhanced details provided by data providers. Additionally, we've introduced a new References tab in the data package. This tab allows you to seamlessly and automatically update emission factor references in consuming apps, ensuring that your apps use the most recent versions. Note that this is currently limited to the apps Manage Purchased Product Footprints and Manage Waste Footprints.

As a prerequisite, confirm that source IDs are stable between versions. If discrepancies exist, you'll need to manually input old and new source IDs in the DatasetUpdates sheet of the import file. Then you can choose the data package ready for an upgrade and proceed to initiate the process:

  1. Input new version details, such as the new validity period, and upload the corresponding import file.
  2. After successful upload, release the new package version.
  3. Go to the References tab and review changes caused by the new version.
  4. Click Update in Apps to apply the new factors.
  5. Confirm or adjust validity periods fetched from the new version and choose Update References.
  6. Review changes meticulously under the References tab, and apply them using the 'Update in Apps' feature.
  7. In case of errors, check consistency messages and resolve the issue.

After successfully updating the references, new line items for the referenced footprint records will be created in the consuming apps, accommodating the new version’s periods. For footprints with overlapping periods, the upgrade shortens the validity of the initial record to match the current version’s period. Check Upgrading to New Data Source Version for more details.

The following demo video gives you an impression of this new feature's capabilities (click on the picture to start the video):

Emission Factor Mapping Mass Updates

 

AI-assisted Emission Factor Mapping Enhancement

The AI-assisted intelligent mapping of ERP product data to emission factors from LCA databases in the Manage Purchased Product Footprints app has been enhanced for more accurate mapping results. The first improvement includes the matching of unit of measure as a mapping criterion. Now, only emission factors that match the product’s reference unit of measure are suggested. Matching occurs when units are identical or convertible within the same dimension, such as mass or time. Quantity conversions are also considered for dimensionless units like piece or each.

To improve the mapping confidence, we’re further introducing new factors in the Similarity Score:

  • Country/Region: Scores are based on the hierarchical distance between the supplier’s country/region and the proposed emission factor, with higher scores for closer regional matches. As example: A product from Germany receives the highest score with an emission factor from Germany, a factor from the European Union results in a lower score and a Rest of World factor in the lowest.
  • Commodity Code: Scores are determined by the number of matching digits between the product's and emission factor's commodity codes. The more matching digits, the higher the score. If no commodity code is maintained, this score is excluded in the calculation of the overall Similarity Score.

These enhancements aim to increase the precision and reliability of mapped emission factors, thereby improving footprint calculation. For details on how to generate emission factor mappings with AI and the logic behind, see Using AI to Map Emission Factors to Purchased Products and Product Groups.

Check the following demo video to learn more about the enhancements (click on the picture to start the video):

AI-assisted Emission Factor Mapping Enhancement

 

Sankey Chart Improvements

The Sankey chart visualization in the Manage Footprint Results app is one of the most powerful tools within SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. It visualizes the flow of emissions from inputs to outputs. This helps you understand your calculated footprints and identify emission hotspots across your products’ value chain. To improve this analyzes further, the chart has been enhanced by categorizing emissions visually into high, medium, and low CO2e levels, aiding quick impact assessment of different items. You can click on an input or output item name within the chart to access a quick view with further insight into CO2e per unit, product quantity, product role (main/by-product), and links to detailed item information. For items with numerous inputs or outputs, the chart displays up to 50 with the most CO2e. Remaining items are grouped under Others, summarizing their total footprint. Arrows next to each item allow navigation to previous or subsequent footprints, making CO2e contributions easier to trace. Additionally, the app's tabs feature a quick filter to identify errors and warnings, and arrows facilitating navigation through item footprints, supporting in visualizing and understanding the primary contributors to CO2e emissions. For more information, see Viewing the Footprint Results.

The following demo video gives you an impression of the improvements (click on the picture to start the video):

Sankey Chart Improvements

 

Internal Energy Production with Multiple Energy Carriers

It’s now possible to model the internal production of energy, such as electricity and heat, in your Energy Flow Model in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. The new feature allows multiple energy carriers to be outputted from a single process infrastructure, even if they’re measured in different units, as long as these units belong to the same dimensional category. The software standardizes all measurements to the unit associated with the process infrastructure's energy carrier to maintain consistency in calculating CO2e emissions. This ensures accurate environmental impact assessments in various output formats. A new UI addition to the valuation formulas in the Manage Footprint Results app now displays the Metered Consumption Quantity (Converted), which is the energy amount recorded by a meter, adjusted to align with the process infrastructure's unit of measure. When a process infrastructure (sender) is linked to meters (energy receivers) with energy carriers in different units of measure but within the same dimension, the meters' energy carrier quantities are converted into the unit of measure of the process infrastructure's energy carrier. This enables CO2e emissions to be allocated across all output items based on the energy carrier's unit of measure. For more information, see the Process Infrastructure section under Model Energy Flows.

 

Item Footprints API

For creating customized reports and dashboards or analyzing calculated footprints in SAP Analytics Cloud or other analytical applications, we published a new API to extract organizational footprint inventories on transactional level. This API complements the existing analytical APIs by extracting your footprints on a granular, item level. Focusing on your Corporate Carbon Footprint, this helps you to evaluate and optimize resources to minimize your footprints. The interface provides information on the Total Footprint and Footprint per Unit for items like Products, Energy Sources, and Facilities, including physical quantities, amounts, and units of measure used in calculations. Additionally, the API offers detailed insights into input and output transactions related to Product, Resource, and Energy footprints, including business transaction types, consumption quantities, amounts, and unit of measure over specified periods. Check out the API and further details on SAP Business Accelerator Hub: Item Footprints.

 

Other Release Highlights

A new feature allows you to model non-energy-relevant resources without planned energy consumption rates. This enables the calculation of production document footprints without errors, even for resources lacking planned energy consumption rates, which aren’t energy relevant. Previously, this resulted in calculation errors. With the new enhancement, using a calculation variant set to Use Only Planned Energy Consumption Rates, non-energy-relevant resources are automatically considered as not requiring energy. They appear on the Sankey chart without a status or CO2e. This replaces prior errors with a warning log in the Monitor Business Logs app, simplifying the process. For more information, see Manage Planned Energy Consumption Rates

To simplify uploading data using Excel files, we’re consistently improving the provided templates. The template for importing waste footprints in the Manage Waste Footprints app now allows a direct mapping to emission factors stored in the Manage Emission Factors app. With the additional fields Package Identifier, Data Source Version, and Dataset ID you can specify the emission factor reference for a waste footprint. Check SAP Help for more details: Importing Waste Footprints. This feature was initially entered as a requirement by customers and partners via SAP Influence and is a great example of how you can directly influence our product development.

We also added improvements to the deletion of data. The Manage Supplier Footprints app now allows you to delete inconsistent supplier footprint records. This enhancement helps you streamline your data and improve data quality. In the Import Master Data app, you can now delete unreferenced master data records for the master data entities Ledgers, Periods, and Period Variants. For more information, see Deleting Unreferenced Records.

Furthermore, a new and enhanced Monitor Business Logs application was added to replace the former app. In this new version, you see two tabs with business log details: Messages and Records. You can also download logs via Export and Export As. For all details on this new app, check SAP Help: Using the Monitor Business Logs Application.

To help you with onboarding and using SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, we’re constantly enhancing the product documentation on SAP Help. In the previous quarter, this included the following topics:

  • Relation between emission factor periods and energy source period: Detailed explanation of the automatic filtering setup when new emission factor periods are added, see Model Energy Flows.
  • Comprehensive examples for updating purchased product footprints: Clear illustrations and practical examples to effectively demonstrate the system's behavior during the updating of purchased product footprints, see Updating Purchased Product Footprints.
  • Delete footprint inventory and results: How to delete footprint inventories and footprint results, see Deleting a Footprint Inventory and Deleting a Footprint Result.
  • Additional processes in the scenario guide: The guide now includes detailed explanations and examples of the processes
    • Process 3 – Calculating Scope 1 & Scope 3.3 Using Planned Energy Consumption Rates
    • Process 4 – Calculating Scope 3.1 Emissions Using Average-Data and Spend-Based Purchased Product Footprints
    • Process 5 – Calculating Scope 3.1 Emissions Using Supplier-Specific Purchased Product Footprints
    • Process 7 – Calculating Scope 3.8, 3.11, 3.13, 3.14 Using Partial Inventory Scope and Allocation Schemes
    • Under Technical Scenarios, we added information about Managing and Monitoring Plausibility Checks for Calculated Carbon Footprints.
  • Glossary of calculation methods for GHG categories: The calculation methods for GHG categories are described in a new Glossary document for reference.

 

Open-Source Integration Accelerator

If you want to integrate SAP Sustainability Footprint Management to SAP ECC or older SAP S/4HANA ERP systems, we have good news: The Integration Accelerator is now available on GitHub, under an open-source license: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Integration Accelerator. As with all open-source content, this is free-of-charge for our customers and partners.

This ABAP package includes prebuilt integration content that simplifies the selection, extraction, and replication of data from ECC systems or older S/4HANA systems into the cloud-based SAP Sustainability Footprint Management solution, using standard Push APIs. Key features of the package include:

  • Preconfigured ABAP Reports: These reports assist in the efficient replication of data.
  • Data Compatibility: Supports integration with both master data and transactional data.
  • Data Load Support: Capable of handling initial and delta data loads, along with single entity reloads.
  • Error Handling and Logging: Provides mechanisms for managing errors and logging processes, including the ability to reprocess failed payloads.
  • Extensibility: Adapt the solution to specific needs and implement custom enhancements through BAdI implementations.

The ABAP code provided can be utilized as is or serve as a reference for custom implementations. For support, it’s recommended to use the existing code, with the possibility of enhancements through BAdI implementation as required.

 

Customer Success Story: HARTING Technology Group

HARTING Technology Group has made significant progress in its sustainability journey by implementing SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. The software allows HARTING to accurately calculate the CO2 emissions of 13,000 materials with a data-granular, verifiable "one-click" solution. This capability is crucial for HARTING as it works towards its ambitious goal of achieving carbon neutrality by 2030.

HARTING, a family-run business and leader in providing connectivity solutions across various industrial sectors, has long integrated environmental and climate protection into its core values. SAP Sustainability Footprint Management leverages their existing SAP ERP application landscape and enhances their ability to drive cleaner production processes in their environmentally friendly facilities. By mitigating greenhouse gas emissions and reducing the impact on water and land life, HARTING is making a tangible difference in its environmental footprint. The company chose the quick-start service for SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, provided by the SAP Services and Support team, to ensure technical and functional readiness for large-scale product deployments. SAP consultants encouraged HARTING to provide product feedback to help scale and enhance the solution by shaping specific functionalities and addressing feature requests.

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management enables HARTING to accurately calculate, analyze, and report product carbon footprints (PCF). The solution provides real-time, granular data supporting HARTING's "GreenLine" label, which highlights the use of biopolymers that offer up to 70% CO2 reduction. Increased transparency throughout the supply chain, with enhanced supplier data contributing to a single source of truth, allows HARTING to quickly identify and address hot spots. The solution's user-friendly, verifiable "one-click" result calculates the CO2 emissions of 13,000 individual product materials. Additionally, data from SAP Sustainability Footprint Management will help HARTING comply with upcoming EU sustainability regulations, such as the Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive and Digital Product Passport. Read the full story here: HARTING Technology Group: Connecting on a greener future.

 

Intermediate Learning Journey for CCF Calculation

Last quarter, we released another Learning Journey: Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Advanced Configurations. This three-hour learning is free-of-charge and targets consultants, business users, and administrators with intermediate product knowledge. The course focuses on integrating SAP Sustainability Footprint Management with ERP systems to effectively calculate and report Corporate Carbon Footprints (CCF). It covers advanced configuration techniques, including managing energy flow models, AI-based emission factor mapping, integrating supplier emission data, and analyzing footprints for waste and sold products. Upon completion, you’ll be equipped to use these advanced methods to calculate CCFs, identify key emission hotspots, and pinpoint levers for decarbonization. You’ll also earn a Record of Achievement and a digital badge after successful completion and passing all quizzes.

Learning Journey: Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Advanced Configuration

For beginners, first complete our Learning Journey Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Simple Configuration. This free, two-hour introductory course covers the Greenhouse Gas Protocol and the functionalities and architecture of SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, demonstrating how to configure the solution to calculate corporate carbon footprints (CCF) across GHG Scopes 1, 2, and 3. The course also explains integrating these calculated footprints into SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

Besides those Learning Journeys focusing on CCF calculation, we’re currently also working on a course centered around Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) calculation in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. Stay tuned!

 

Additional Information

For in-depth details on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, including concepts, setup, and the application help, I recommend you browsing through the SAP Help Portal. For an overview of all new features and functions that have been released, check the What’s New section, which is updated with every new software release.

For exploring and experimenting in a live system, register for the free SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Trial. You’ll receive an e-mail with access information within seconds after your registration and can then test the solution at your own convenience within a 30-day trial period. It also includes a wide range of guided tours designed to help you navigate through all the features and functions comprehensively. Recently, we also added new tours and will shortly release additional ones, representing the latest features and an enhanced user experience.

To stay up to date on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, follow me here in the SAP Community. In regular cadence, I’ll publish blog posts giving you updates on all the product’s new features and functions as well as deep dives into the product capabilities. I’m also looking forward to engaging with you directly, feel free to leave your feedback in the comment section.

entry.title: SAP Screen Personas and Adapt UI simplify classic apps. April 2025 roadmap update
entry.content:
Customer adoption of SAP Screen Personas and Adapt UI for classic apps within SAP S/4HANA Cloud continues to grow. The most popular scenarios in which people simplify the classic screens in public cloud environments are sales orders and purchase orders. In this post, I’ll discuss when to use SAP Screen Personas and SAP Fiori apps, the SAP Screen Personas roadmap, and some specific next steps for customers and partners to optimize your UX transformation journey.

SAP Screen Personas is one way to deliver the SAP Fiori user experience

In a recent call with a partner supporting a large retailer, they asked if their customer should use SAP Screen Personas to simplify the classic sales order apps or to use the latest SAP Fiori app for Manage Sales Orders. Generally, if there is an SAP Fiori app that SAP ships, we recommend you evaluate it. SAP Fiori apps based on SAP Fiori elements receive ongoing innovations, including embedded AI capabilities that continue to evolve.

Most organizations will use a combination of SAP-delivered Fiori apps, adapted SAP Fiori apps, SAP Screen Personas flavors for classic applications, and SAP Fiori elements apps that they build themselves to provide a complete and consistent UX to their users. So, you should probably use a combination of both SAP Screen Personas AND SAP Fiori.

SAP Screen Personas is one of several steps that SAP has taken over the past decade to make its ERP screen easier to use:

  • Creating 1000s of SAP Fiori apps to cover the most common business scenarios
  • Making SAP Screen Personas available to help customers simplify other transactions
  • Acquiring WalkMe to allow customers to annotate workflows in enterprise applications

Continuous improvement is part of the SAP UX Engineering culture

Within SAP Screen Personas, we are continuing to innovate to make it easier for you to streamline business processes that still rely on classic apps. In addition to all the basic screen editing functions that have been available for over a decade, we are making scripting more powerful and providing more capabilities to manage the tables that are so common in classic apps. With all the innovations in the SAP Fiori design system, we have also added more granular controls so you can give the classic screens the SAP Fiori user experience to improve usability and ensure consistency across the entire SAP portfolio.

SAP Screen Personas flavors can launch from My Home, alongside SAP Fiori apps, again providing a consistent way to access core ERP functionality. In short, the SAP Screen Personas roadmap focuses on continuing to innovate with new features and integrate into the rest of the portfolio so you can enjoy better usability and more UX consistency in your SAP ERP systems.

VA01, VA02, VA03, ME21N, ME22N, ME23N remain among the most common transactions

SAP Screen Personas is a key part of SAP’s user experience strategy

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – the ability to simplify classic screens is built into the Adapt UI menu. If you open it for an SAP Fiori app, you can edit the screen with SAPUI5 Flexibility. If you open Adapt UI in a classic app, you will launch the SAP Screen Personas screen editor.
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition – the add-on is available for download.
  • SAP S/4HANA (on-prem) and ECC – the process is the same as for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, since you control your IT environment.

Since we added it to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as a standard feature, that should be solid evidence that the ability to adapt the UI of classic apps is something important to us. We are supporting SAP Screen Personas functionality through at least 2040.

SAP Screen Persons flavors are part of a clean core strategy, as they do not touch the core transactions / classic apps.

How to improve the user experience of your SAP ERP system

Customers

  • If you run SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP Fiori apps should be your go-to solution, whenever possible. The SAP Fiori app reference library has a list of all the apps that are available in your environment.
  • For classic applications that don’t have an SAP Fiori app, you can use the power of SAP Screen Personas to hide fields you don’t need, pre-fill content if the information does not change, or automate repetitive actions using the scripting engine.
  • SAP Screen Personas is built into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as part of the Adapt UI capabilities. For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-prem, you can install the add-on in less than an hour, if you know what you are doing. There is no additional license fee to use SAP Screen Personas in any environment.

Partners

SAP Screen Personas should be one of several key tools that are part of your UX transformation tool kit. If you don’t have expertise today, you can start with the free training course on SAP Screen Personas on the SAP Learning platform.

Learn more about SAP Screen Personas

  • Online training. The best way to learn the basics about SAP Screen Personas (Adapt UI for classic applications) is to take our course on the SAP Learning platform. https://learning.sap.com/courses/adapting-the-ui-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-using-sap-screen-personas
  • Connect with your peers. Every other month, we have a group of customers that gather online to share best practices, demo their flavors, and answer each other’s questions. Experts from the product team also join to explain how to use the latest functionality. If you are interested in joining, you can sign up online to join the SAP Screen Personas practitioner forum. This will add you to the mailing list for the monthly meeting summaries. Please contact me directly if you want to join the monthly calls.

I hope you start using Adapt UI for Classic Apps or SAP Screen Personas to make your business users happier and more productive.

For the SAP UX Foundations team, Peter Spielvogel.
(Yes, this is a new name for our organization. Same mission, same goals. The new name reflects that we have product managers and UX experts working together with engineers to achieve our goal of delighting SAP customers with a better user experience in SAP S/4HANA.)

entry.title: SDT Using SAP Business Transformation Center: End to End Process & Technical Configuration
entry.content:
Introduction:

SAP Business Transformation Center is a SaaS (software-as-a-service) product from SAP, embedded into SAP Cloud ALM. It is a one-stop shop for customers who want to selectively transition from SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ERP) 6.08 to SAP S/4HANA 2023 or beyond using through SHELL CONVERSION method of SDT. Their target can be either to S/4 HANA on-premises or SAP S/4 HANA private cloud edition under RISE. In general, it is available to all the customer with a valid SAP Service Cloud subscriptions and the SAP Enterprise Support agreement and does not involve any additional license cost for usage.

In this blog i will show you End-to-End migration process and setup the connectivity between source and target system with SAP Cloud ALM.

Technical System Landscape:

SAP Business Transformation Center is a cloud native application which relies on the following components:
